text: In the era of digital transformation, blockchain technology provides a solution to the problems faced by interoperability, data security and resource utilization in the existing systems. Blockchain technology, as a decentralized system, has the potential to revolutionize the existing healthcare system. Therefore, this study aims at identifying the change brought about by the use of blockchain technology in the medical and healthcare sector and how it acts as a change strategy to overcome the structural barriers of healthcare. Based on the PRISMA guidelines, this systematic review reviewed ten core studies relating to the development and implementation of blockchain in healthcare. The core studies were based on the applications of blockchain in providing data protection, anti-counterfeit drug tracking in pharmaceutical industry and safe healthcare information exchange among the hospitals. This paper provides an integrated summary of the current applications and developments of blockchain technology in healthcare systems, examining the clinical and managerial benefits and challenges associated with its adoption at the health system level.
